title: Choppin’ It Up: TGI Friday CEO’s Plans for Top-Line Growth
summary: TGI Friday’s is eliminating busy work from their general manager’s plates, allowing them to focus on the team members, Sugarloaf Holdings Founder and CEO Ray Blanchette tells Bloomberg Intelligence. In this episode of the Choppin’ It Up podcast, Blanchette sits down with BI’s senior restaurant and foodservice analyst Michael Halen to discuss TGI Friday’s focus on four-wall execution, hospitality and food quality to boost US same-store sales. Blanchette also comments on value, international store

title: Podcast: Tokenization, Digital Twins and the Future of ETFs
summary: On this episode of Trillions, we explore how this government transformation will affect stocks, bonds and exchange-traded funds.

title: Chip Stocks Hit by AI Spending Worries After Rally: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations, with a resurgence in geopolitical risks also weighing on sentiment.

title: Citi Traders Pitch Shorting French Shipping Giant CMA CGM Bonds
summary: Citigroup’s credit traders and analysts are recommending their hedge fund clients short bonds issued by French shipping giant CMA CGM Group, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Paschi Casts Doubts Over Intesa Bid, Suggests Studying BPM Plan
summary: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA’s board of directors raised broad concerns over a takeover bid by Intesa Sanpaolo SpA, saying the price offered appears low and that it relies on optimistic assumptions while exposing shareholders to risks.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil steadied, largely holding onto a run of gains with concerns mounting about disruptions to flows from the Middle East as US strikes against Iran intensify.

title: Chevron to Sign Oil Investment Accords With Iraq on Friday
summary: Chevron Corp. expects to sign accords with Iraq on Friday that will advance its negotiations about investing in large oil fields, according to a senior company executive.

title: Spain Extends Welcome Mat for Chinese Carmakers to Its Workers
summary: Spain’s biggest Chinese industrial investment will rely on workers brought in from China during construction, according to a government report that lays out Madrid’s blueprint to bolster its auto industry.

title: Ladbrokes Owner Entain to Cut 2% of Staff as UK Taxes Hit
summary: Entain Plc will cut 500 jobs, or about 2% of its global workforce, as the gambling industry grapples with higher UK taxes, tighter regulation and growing competition from prediction markets.

title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
summary: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a decline in assets over the past three years.

title: Balbec Secures $930 Million in Commitments for Asset-Based Credit Fund
summary: Alternative asset manager Balbec Capital LP has obtained commitments of more than $930 million for its latest flagship fund that will invest in a variety of asset-based credit, according to a filing on Thursday.

title: India’s Maruti to Fight Order to Replace Car Over Ethanol Damage
summary: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. said it will challenge a consumer court order asking the nation’s largest carmaker to replace a vehicle over alleged damage from ethanol-blended fuel, a case that assumes significance as the debate over the nation’s biofuel push intensifies.

title: Wheat Wavers After Touching Two-Year High Amid Black Sea Strikes
summary: Wheat fluctuated after touching a two-year high as traders weighed ongoing Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea and weak US export data.

title: Aston Martin Creditors Tap Jefferies as Debt Worries Grow
summary: A group of creditors to Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc have picked Jefferies Financial Group Inc. as financial adviser as concern over the debt of the distressed British automaker grows, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Bank of America Exits Chicago Plan to Sell Overdue Parking Debt
summary: Bank of America is no longer working with Chicago on the sale of debt owed to the city from fines and fees, and officials have been unable to advance the deal so far this year, according to the mayor’s office.

title: Bond Traders Bail on Fed Hike Wagers on Softer Inflation Outlook
summary: The bullish tone in the US bond market following two benign inflation reports has traders of interest-rate options scrambling to exit positions bought in anticipation of at least one increase by the Federal Reserve this year.

title: What Nigeria Stocks Tell Investors About Africa Markets: Live Q&A
summary: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about Nigeria’s world-beating stocks, and where investors are finding opportunities in African markets, in a Live Q&A on Friday, July 17 at 12 p.m. GMT

title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
summary: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.

title: Emerging Currencies Dip as Oil, Dollar Spike Over Iran Worries
summary: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as renewed worries about tensions in the Middle East lifted oil prices in another risk-off session in global markets.

title: Faena Condo Project Sells $30 Million Penthouse on Miami River
summary: Argentine fashion entrepreneur-turned-developer Alan Faena sold a $30 million penthouse in his planned twin condo towers on the Miami River, a milestone in the mission to take his maximalist, theatrical brand worldwide.

title: Jump Trading Doubles Team to Ride Record Prediction Market Boom
summary: As prediction markets draw record trading around events like the World Cup, Jump Trading Group is betting the once-niche contracts are becoming a lasting corner of Wall Street.
